response.justification: Bogdan has a genuine geospatial background through GISCollective and Green Map work, but his career is overwhelmingly TypeScript/Node.js/web stack. The role demands modern commercial C++ or Rust as the primary competency — his C++ exposure is limited to a 2014–2015 stint at National Instruments and a bachelor thesis project, with no subsequent systems-level work. His distributed systems experience comes from high-throughput web microservices (Sociomantic), not routing data pipelines or navigation tile generation. The geospatial work, while present, centers on data collection and visualization for NGOs rather than the performance-critical, latency-sensitive routing tile processing the role requires.
strengths: Hands-on geospatial platform experience via GISCollective — event-driven geo-data platform used in production by real organizations
Demonstrated distributed systems exposure at Sociomantic handling millions of HTTP requests/second with DLang async I/O
Linux server application background and strong open-source contribution history
concerns: No current commercial C++ or Rust experience — primary stack is TypeScript/Node.js; C++ work is over a decade old and non-routing in scope
Geospatial work is web GIS / data collection, not routing tile generation, map data transformation pipelines, or navigation guidance systems
No evidence of working with routing algorithms, tile formats, real-time traffic integration, or the performance constraints central to this role
